data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Amazon uses a microservices architecture to allow for independent deployment and scaling.
data.technical_spec: Decomposes applications into smaller, independent services that communicate over APIs.

data.title: AWS Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Amazon.com runs on Amazon Web Services, utilizing a vast cloud infrastructure.
data.technical_spec: Leverages AWS products such as EC2, S3, and RDS for scalability and reliability.

data.title: Multi-tier Architecture
data.description: The site uses a multi-tier architecture involving front-end, application logic, and data storage layers.
data.technical_spec: Separates concerns into presentation, application, and data storage layers.

data.title: Service-Oriented Architecture
data.description: Supports integration and communication between services across Amazon’s platforms.
data.technical_spec: Uses SOA principles to enhance reusability and manageability.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Amazon employs load balancing to ensure efficient distribution of incoming requests.
data.technical_spec: Uses load balancers to direct traffic across multiple servers to maintain performance.
